title: Virginia Colony makes male sodomy a capital offense
description: The Colony of Virginia issued a military order that criminalized sex between men and set death as the penalty. The order lapsed later the same year when martial law ended after a change of control in the colony.

sources.label: Medium — A Comprehensive Queer History Timeline of the United States

title: Richard Cornish hanged for sodomy in Virginia Colony
description: Richard Williams Cornish of the Virginia Colony was tried and executed by hanging on a charge of sodomy, one of the earliest recorded such prosecutions in the North American colonies.

sources.label: Medium — A Comprehensive Queer History Timeline of the United States

title: Connecticut Colony sodomy law covers sex between women
description: The Connecticut Colony enacted a sodomy statute that also prescribed punishment for sexual acts between women, an unusually explicit reference to lesbian conduct for colonial-era law.

sources.label: Medium — A Comprehensive Queer History Timeline of the United States

title: Sarah Norman charged in first known lesbian prosecution
description: In Plymouth, Massachusetts, Sarah White Norman was charged with lewd conduct with Mary Vincent Hammon, the earliest documented prosecution for sexual activity between women in North America.

sources.label: Medium — A Comprehensive Queer History Timeline of the United States
